## 📝 Executive Summary

## UniSan S2P — Green

**Overall score:** 0.04/1.00 | Forward risk: 0.06 | Historical slip: 0.00

The project is rated **Green**. There are 42 Red tasks and 9 Yellow/Amber tasks
across the plan. 149 tasks are completed; 42 are currently in progress.

Monte Carlo simulation (n=10,000) gives **4%** probability of finishing by 2026-10-09. 
**Top drivers (model importance):** total_float_days, variance_sign_code, pct_complete.



**Risk concentration:** 51% of active risk tasks are primarily owned by 'UniSan Integration Team' (Area/Phase not populated in source data — clustered on Owner, Task Name).

**Recommendation:** Focus immediate attention on the 2 Red task(s) on the critical path (out of 42 Red tasks total across the plan).

> **Schedule risk note:** Task-level RAG health is mostly Green, but Monte Carlo analysis based on completion throughput gives only **4%** probability of meeting the 2026-10-09 (30 weeks of remaining work at current throughput). Median forecast finish: 2026-12-06. The risk is structural — the team is on track task-by-task but significantly behind on timeline. **Escalate schedule risk to sponsor.**


*ℹ️ Rule-based narrative (LLM unavailable or key not set)*

## 🎲 Monte Carlo Deadline Forecast

| Model | Basis | P(on-time) | Median Finish |
|-------|-------|-----------|---------------|
| Throughput (v1, baseline) | Completion rate × log-normal variance | **4%** | 2026-12-06 |
| Dependency-aware DAG (v2) | Topological propagation through predecessor graph | **7%** | 2026-10-16 |

**Deadline:** 2026-10-09

> *Dependency-aware model is 3 pp higher than throughput baseline — parallel predecessor paths absorb some schedule risk visible in throughput extrapolation.*

> *v1 caveat: Based on single-snapshot data. Completion rate: 7.7 tasks/week from 149 completed tasks. Remaining: 234 active tasks. Duration ratio (log-normal) fitted from 73 tasks with parseable dates. No weekly history or dependency graph available — treat as directional.*

> *v2 caveat: Graph covers 282/383 tasks (74% have predecessor data). Tasks without predecessors start immediately (treated as roots). 18 non-FS edge(s) (6% of edges) simplified to FS+lag [types: FF, SS]. 40 task(s) used dataset-mean duration (planned_days missing). No resource leveling applied.*

## 📊 Naïve vs Model Baseline

| View | Signal | Verdict |
|------|--------|---------|​
| **Naïve (% complete vs time)** | 8% tasks done; 62% of schedule elapsed | Behind — 8% done, 62% of time elapsed (54pp gap) |
| **This agent — RAG + ML** | Score 0.039; 2/50 critical-path tasks Red | **Green** |
| **This agent — Monte Carlo** | 10,000 simulations, historical duration ratios from completed tasks | P(on-time) = 4% → 7% after dependency-graph correction (DAG v2) |

> *The naïve view ignores velocity trends, critical-path composition, and duration variance. The agent's MC uses historical slip rates from completed tasks and propagates them through the predecessor DAG — producing a calibrated probability rather than a binary on/off.*
